summary: The case describes daytime meal omission with subsequent late-night hunger and impulsive food choices, accompanied by a perceived breakdown in hunger cues. Modifiable levers span nutrition/biological regulation, cognitive-behavioral strategies, and social/environmental supports. The proposed solutions aim to 1) re-anchor daytime eating through planning, reminders, and accountability; 2) recalibrate hunger signaling via interoception training and education; 3) stabilize nocturnal eating by pre-planned, healthier options and sleep-circadian alignment; 4) leverage digital tools and social supports to maintain consistent routines; and 5) reduce nighttime temptations with environmental tweaks. Each lever maps to specific criteria (C01–C06), includes concrete measurement metrics, and emphasizes feasibility and expected impact within a 4–12 week horizon.

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement scheduled daytime meals with automated reminders (phone/app notifications) and a simple daily meal plan template, including a pre-commitment to eat at set times.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Frequent daytime meal initiation reduces prolonged fasting, stabilizes energy, and improves attention to meals, lowering nocturnal hunger drive and impulsive night choices.
solutions.measurement: Number of daytime meals per day; time to first meal after waking; missed daytime meals per day; proportion of days meeting at least 2 meals by 2pm.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to C01 and C06; utilizes Meal_Planning_and_Structure (ID:43) and Digital_Tools_and_Supports (ID:112).

solutions.solution_candidate: Provide daily short interoception exercises to detect hunger/fullness cues, paired with mindful eating practices (5–10 minutes daily) and appetite journaling.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved accuracy of internal hunger cues reduces signals misalignment and may decrease late-night overeating.
solutions.measurement: Cue accuracy ratings (hunger/fullness vs. intake); daily hunger cue correlation with meals; nights with impulsive eating episodes.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to C04; aligns with Interoception/Mindfulness components (CBT/ACT-inspired).

solutions.solution_candidate: Plan and prep energy-dense but nutritious evening options; stock healthier items; implement pre-commitment to chosen options to avoid impulsive choices.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Having readily available healthy alternatives reduces reliance on impulsive, energy-dense choices during late-evening hunger surges.
solutions.measurement: No. of nights with planned vs. unplanned snacks; late-night snack calories; nocturnal hunger episodes per week.
solutions.mapping_query: Links to C02 and C03; uses Meal_Planning_and_Structure (ID:43) and Eating_Behavior_and_Satiety_Skills (ID:41).

solutions.solution_candidate: Provide a protein-rich pre-sleep snack (20–30g) to stabilize overnight hunger and reduce late-night cravings.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Protein at night enhances satiety hormones and buffers hunger signals, reducing nocturnal eating and poor choices.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of late-night snacks; overnight hunger intensity ratings; days with a pre-sleep protein snack.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to C02 and C03; Nutrition_Tools (ID:41, 44).

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent bed/wake times, morning light exposure, and a calming pre-sleep routine to align circadian rhythms.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Circadian alignment improves appetite hormone regulation and enhances reliability of hunger cues.
solutions.measurement: Bed/wake time consistency (hours); average sleep duration; nights with hunger episodes.
solutions.mapping_query: Links to C02 and C04; Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration (ID:60-64).

solutions.solution_candidate: Assess and optimize hydration (water intake) and micronutrient status (iron, B12, magnesium) to support energy and appetite regulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Hydration and micronutrient adequacy influence energy, cognitive function, and hunger signal processing.
solutions.measurement: Daily water intake (ml); basic micronutrient status indicators (if available); daytime meals per day; hunger cue reliability ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: ID42 Hydration; ID47 Micronutrient; Maps to C04, C01.

solutions.solution_candidate: Engage a meal buddy or family member to schedule and participate in daytime meals; leverage social accountability to reduce meal skipping.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social cues and accountability promote adherence to daytime meals and daily structure.
solutions.measurement: Days with meals due to social support; adherence rate to scheduled meals; self-reported social engagement around meals.

solutions.solution_candidate: Use a digital app to log meals, hunger levels, and cravings; provide automated feedback and optional sharing with a coach or support network.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Self-monitoring with feedback enhances awareness and habit formation; social sharing boosts accountability.
solutions.measurement: Proportion of days with meals logged; correlation between hunger ratings and meals; adherence to meal timing.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to Self_Assessment_and_Logs (ID50) and Digital_Tools (ID112); targets C01, C04, C06.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach urge surfing and 5–10 minute delay techniques, plus implementation intentions for nighttime snacking.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances executive control over late-night urges, reducing nocturnal overeating and impulsive choices.
solutions.measurement: No. of successful delay attempts; frequency of late-night snacks; self-rated urge strength.

solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver psychoeducation modules about hunger vs appetite and how to respond to cues.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better understanding reduces misinterpretation of signals and supports appropriate meal timing and choices.
solutions.measurement: Knowledge score on hunger cues; meal timing adherence; self-reported hunger-signal confidence.
solutions.mapping_query: PSYCHO - Psychoeducation modules (ID116) and related CBT resources.

solutions.solution_candidate: Modify the nighttime environment to reduce snack cues: store snacks out of reach, remove in-bed temptations, and set default non-eating routines.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Environmental cues strongly influence nighttime eating; reducing cues supports routine integrity.
solutions.measurement: No. of nighttime snacks; time to last snack; self-reported snack temptations.
